name: Visualization / demo layer
role: The pitch: show the learning delta — UNTRAINED cars gridlock/crash vs the TRAINED coordination policy flowing smoothly with zero collisions, on the real SF map.
decision: Decide the renderer now — it's the demo. Baseline = the UNTRAINED coordination policy (the 'today's traffic' shadow world), NOT a separate traffic sim. Two render paths: (1) PRIMARY/cinematic = Isaac RTX render of the rigid-body cars (high-level policy -> frozen low-level controller); (2) ALWAYS-WORKS fallback = log trajectories (car_id,t,x,y,heading) and render with deck.gl TripsLayer on a Mapbox SF basemap. Build the deck.gl path first so you always have a demo, then layer Isaac on top if the box holds.
inputs: trained coordination-policy trajectories (from kinematic-env and/or Isaac)
outputs: two-worlds overlay: untrained (low opacity, crashes/gridlock) vs trained (full opacity, smooth)
live reward-component counters (crashes=0, idle-time down, detour)
design_notes: The baseline is FREE and HONEST: just run the policy at an early/zero checkpoint -> real gridlock/crashes. The delta to the trained policy IS the story.
Render OFFLINE from logged trajectories — do not render live inside the training loop.
deck.gl TripsLayer is purpose-built for animated vehicle trajectories on a map; sells 'real San Francisco'.
Isaac render = the cinematic upgrade (real physics), gated on the RTX/L40S box.
risks: under-budgeting demo polish (it's the whole pitch)
depending solely on Isaac -> build the deck.gl fallback first
